summary:
The most likely pathophysiology is thrombin inhibitor deficiency, specifically protein C or S deficiency, given their established role in causing recurrent venous thrombosis in young patients with familial tendencies. This contrasts with outdated concerns about high homocysteine levels or autoimmune conditions lacking supportive features in her case.

Thrombin inhibitor deficiency, especially protein C or S deficiency, is the most plausible cause because it is linked to recurrent venous thrombosis in young females with genetic susceptibility.
